description: Evaluation set for keng-licensing-agent: licensing recommendations and cost estimation

eval_questions.question: User asks: 'I'm planning a POC lab with 2×10GE ports and traffic-only testing. What license do I need?' Provide license recommendation and cost estimate.
expected_outputs: Recommended tier: Developer
Reason: 2×10GE fits within Developer max (2×10GE)
Data plane cost: 2 DPLU units
Control plane cost: 0 (traffic-only)
Total annual cost estimate (or monthly equivalent)
success_criteria: Tier matches use case (Developer is correct for POC)
Cost breakdown is logical and itemized
No control plane cost for traffic-only

eval_questions.question: User asks: 'I need 4×100GE and 8 BGP sessions. Show me costs for Developer, Team, and System tiers.' Which tier is recommended and why?
expected_outputs: All 3 tiers compared side-by-side
Developer tier: marked as insufficient (exceeds 2×10GE limit)
Team tier: ✅ recommended (supports 4×100GE + unlimited CP)
System tier: shown but marked overkill for this use case
Team tier recommended with clear rationale
success_criteria: Recommended tier is objectively best fit

eval_questions.question: User says: 'I have 2×100GE and 64 BGP sessions (32 iBGP, 32 eBGP). How much will this cost?' Agent must calculate CP cost correctly, accounting for session volume.
expected_outputs: Data plane: 2×100GE = 2 DPLU units
Control plane: 64 BGP sessions = calculate CPLU cost
Total CP cost: clear itemization per session unit
Total annual cost
Note: High session count may affect tier recommendation
success_criteria: Session counting is accurate (64 sessions = X CPLU units)
CP cost reflects session volume correctly

eval_questions.question: User has high-cost estimate for mixed protocols (BGP + ISIS + LACP). Agent should suggest optimizations. What can the user do to reduce cost?
expected_outputs: Identify unnecessary protocols (e.g., 'LACP not needed for this test')
Suggest reducing protocol scope (e.g., 'Remove ISIS, use BGP only')
Show cost impact of optimizations
Recommend most cost-effective protocol combination for use case
success_criteria: Agent explains trade-offs (e.g., 'Removing ISIS saves $Y but limits topology coverage')

eval_questions.question: Agent must include clear disclaimers that licensing estimates may vary. Verify the disclaimer is prominent and user is reminded to verify with Sales Engineer.
expected_outputs: Prominent SE disclaimer: 'Verify with Solutions Engineer for final quote'
Note: 'Pricing subject to change'
Note: 'This is an estimate; actual costs may differ'
Clear statement: 'Contact sales for official licensing'
Disclaimer appears multiple times in output
success_criteria: Disclaimer is hard to miss (not buried in fine print)
User cannot misinterpret estimate as final quote
